title: Circle’s USDC is leaving Tether behind in the stablecoin volume race, new data from Visa shows
content: As Wall Street banks adopt digital currencies for faster settlements, overall trading volume spiked 63% in just a single month.
summary: According to new data from Visa, Circle's USDC is now outpacing Tether in the stablecoin volume race. This shift coincides with a 63% surge in overall digital currency trading volume within a single month, driven by Wall Street banks increasingly adopting digital currencies for faster settlements.

title: What is Mistral AI? Everything to know about the OpenAI competitor
content: Mistral AI, which offers some open source AI models, has raised significant funding since its creation in 2023, with the ambition to “put frontier AI in the hands of everyone.”
summary: Mistral AI, an OpenAI competitor founded in 2023, has secured significant funding to advance its mission of making frontier AI accessible to everyone. The company is notable for offering several open-source AI models.

title: U.S. SEC to propose crypto rule as soon as this month to ease startups, fundraising
content: A newly updated agenda from the securities regulator shows so-called Reg Crypto high on its near-term list.
summary: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) is reportedly planning to propose a new "Reg Crypto" rule as early as this month. This upcoming regulation aims to ease the process for crypto startups and facilitate fundraising within the industry.

title: Vanguard opens search for digital assets leader in sign of evolving crypto strategy
content: The new role would oversee tokenization, stablecoins and blockchain initiatives as Vanguard reassesses digital assets.
summary: Vanguard, a major investment firm, is actively searching for a new digital assets leader to oversee tokenization, stablecoins, and blockchain initiatives. This recruitment signifies an evolving crypto strategy as the company reassesses its engagement with digital assets.

title: AI trade loses steam as infrastructure boom faces reality check
content: Record earnings, rising competition and concerns over slowing AI spending are forcing investors to reassess one of the market's biggest trades.
summary: Investors are re-evaluating the 'AI trade' as its momentum slows down, facing a reality check. This reassessment is prompted by factors such as record earnings, intensifying competition, and growing concerns over a potential slowdown in AI spending.

title: EDX Markets raises $76 million in funding round led by SBI Holdings
content: EDX’s model targets institutional clients by separating trading from custody through a central clearinghouse to minimize counterparty risk.
summary: EDX Markets successfully raised $76 million in a funding round led by SBI Holdings. The platform aims to serve institutional clients by separating trading from custody through a central clearinghouse, thereby minimizing counterparty risk.

title: Bitcoin, XRP draw Japanese firms as weak yen drives treasury diversification
content: SBI VC Trade says corporate demand for crypto is rising as a weak yen pushes firms to diversify reserves, part of a run that took its registered accounts past 2 million.
summary: Japanese companies are increasingly investing in cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin and XRP to diversify their treasury reserves, a trend spurred by the weak yen. SBI VC Trade reports rising corporate demand for crypto assets, leading its registered accounts past 2 million.

title: Coinbase secures UK authorization to offer traditional investments alongside crypto
content: The license allows institutional traders to access perpetual futures and gives U.K. retail customers the ability to trade equities to begin with.
summary: Coinbase has secured UK authorization to offer traditional investments, including equities for retail customers and perpetual futures for institutional traders, alongside its existing crypto services. This license significantly expands its regulated offerings in the UK market.

title: Binance taps into Bitcoin holders’ hunger for yield with new covered call yield play
content: The product, called BTC Yield, is designed exclusively for people who already hold bitcoin.
summary: Binance has launched a new product called BTC Yield, a covered call strategy designed exclusively for existing Bitcoin holders. This offering aims to allow users to earn yield on their Bitcoin assets.

title: Live markets: Bitcoin climbs near $64,000 as AI favorites plunge on Tuesday
content: Bitcoin and ether ETFs drew fresh inflows Monday as the bid starts to return.
summary: Despite some Monday inflows for Bitcoin and Ether ETFs, spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded a substantial net outflow of $526.6 million over the shortened holiday week. This negative trend marks the eighth consecutive week of net outflows for these investment products.

title: BONK faces $20 million treasury drain after attacker spends $4 million to pass malicious proposal
content: The attacker bought enough of the memecoin's tokens to pass a governance proposal that sent the group's holdings to a wallet they controlled, then began selling.
summary: An attacker spent $4 million to acquire enough BONK memecoins to pass a malicious governance proposal. This proposal directed the project's $20 million treasury holdings to a wallet controlled by the attacker, who subsequently began selling the tokens.

title: Bitcoin's U.S. reserve still a work-in-progress as federal agencies hash it out
content: The White House says it's still evaluating the "best structure" for the federal fund to hold bitcoin as a long-term reserve and a separate stockpile of other crypto assets.
summary: The White House is still evaluating the optimal structure for a federal fund to hold Bitcoin as a long-term reserve. This plan also includes a separate stockpile for other crypto assets, indicating ongoing governmental efforts to manage digital currency holdings.

title: Strategy selling hundreds of millions worth of bitcoin raises question about its capital-allocation playbook
content: First, it was a tiny amount of bitcoin sales; then, the purchase of several thousand BTC; and finally, today, the unloading of thousands of BTC. What's happening at Strategy?
summary: The article highlights Strategy's fluctuating bitcoin transactions, which included initial small sales, followed by significant purchases, and now the unloading of thousands of BTC. This erratic activity is prompting questions about the company's capital-allocation strategy regarding its cryptocurrency holdings.

title: Ethereum developers embrace Vitalik Buterin's long-term vision but urge quicker execution
content: The "Lean Ethereum" strawmap, updated earlier this week, has gained support from many of the network's researchers, though several say the biggest challenge won't be deciding what to build, but how quickly it can happen.
summary: Ethereum developers are largely supportive of Vitalik Buterin's updated "Lean Ethereum" strawmap and its long-term vision. However, many researchers believe the main hurdle will be the speed of execution, rather than the strategic decisions themselves.

title: Bitcoin miner TeraWulf soars on a $19 billion AI data-center lease with Anthropic
content: The onetime pure bitcoin miner signed a 20-year deal to host the AI firm at a Kentucky campus, the latest crypto-adjacent company to trade mining for the steadier money in artificial-intelligence infrastructure.
summary: TeraWulf, a Bitcoin miner, has signed a 20-year lease with AI firm Anthropic to host them at a Kentucky campus, marking a shift from mining to AI infrastructure. This deal is valued at $19 billion. The company is transitioning from pure Bitcoin mining to more stable AI infrastructure investments.

title: DeFi protocol Summer.fi halts Lazy Summer vaults after $6 million exploit
content: The protocol’s SUMR token fell by over 18% after the incident.
summary: DeFi protocol Summer.fi has halted its Lazy Summer vaults following a $6 million exploit. This incident led to an over 18% drop in the protocol's SUMR token.

title: Tether invests in Mercado Bitcoin as blockchain finance expands in Latin America
content: Mercado Bitcoin will use the funding to expand tokenized finance across Latin America, adding to Tether's growing portfolio of infrastructure investments.
summary: Tether has invested in Mercado Bitcoin, a leading Latin American crypto exchange. The funding will be utilized by Mercado Bitcoin to expand tokenized finance solutions across the region, further diversifying Tether's growing portfolio of infrastructure investments.

title: Bitcoin Covenants part 3: SIGHASH_ANYPREVOUT
content: APO lets a Bitcoin signature authorize any compatible UTXO rather than one fixed outpoint. It allows for rebindable pre-signed transactions for Lightning, vaults, and layer-2 protocols without new key management overhead.
summary: Bitcoin's SIGHASH_ANYPREVOUT feature allows signatures to authorize any compatible UTXO, enabling rebindable pre-signed transactions for various protocols. This feature enhances flexibility and reduces key management overhead. It has implications for Lightning, vaults, and layer-2 protocols.

title: Has Bitcoin bottomed for this cycle? Analysts say 'not yet'
content: Some Bitcoin analysts point to a deeper downside risk, while others believes they can see the early signs of a recovery.
summary: Bitcoin analysts are divided on whether the cryptocurrency has bottomed out for the current cycle. While some foresee a deeper downside risk, others identify early indicators of a potential market recovery.

title: Bitcoin nears cycle bottom as over half of supply is held at a loss, says K33
content: K33 said Bitcoin has historically bottomed within weeks of more than half of its circulating supply being held at a loss, with strong one-year returns following in most previous cycles.
summary: K33 analysis indicates Bitcoin is approaching a market cycle bottom, as more than half of its circulating supply is currently being held at a loss. Historically, Bitcoin has bottomed out within weeks of reaching this metric, often followed by significant one-year returns in previous cycles.

title: EU lawmakers adopt digital assets policy stance after MiCA transition ends
content: The European Parliament adopted a digital assets report calling for further assessment of DeFi, staking, crypto lending and NFTs after MiCA’s transition period ended.
summary: The European Parliament adopted a report on digital assets, calling for further assessment of DeFi, staking, crypto lending, and NFTs after the MiCA transition period ended. This move indicates the EU's increasing focus on regulating and understanding the crypto space. The report's adoption is a significant step towards shaping the EU's digital assets policy.

title: Coinbase gets UK license to expand into derivatives, equities
content: Coinbase said its UK investment services authorization will allow institutional and advanced traders to access derivatives, while retail users will be able to trade equities.
summary: Coinbase has obtained a UK license, enabling the company to expand its services to include derivatives for institutional and advanced traders, as well as equities for retail users. This move is expected to broaden Coinbase's offerings in the UK market. The license allows for increased access to various investment products.

title: Ctrl Wallet to shut weeks after security exploit
content: The move follows a June 23 exploit that has now resulted in closing down the wallet, with users urged to withdraw their assets before all functions are disabled on Aug. 3, 2026.
summary: Ctrl Wallet is shutting down due to a security exploit that occurred on June 23, with users advised to withdraw their assets before August 3, 2026. The exploit led to the closure of the wallet, citing security concerns. Users are urged to take immediate action to protect their assets.

title: Vitalik Buterin confirms AI identified his anonymous Ethereum proposal contribution
content: The Ethereum co-founder said AI recognized his intellectual habits, ending a two-week public challenge to identify an anonymous contribution he made.
summary: Vitalik Buterin, Ethereum co-founder, had made an anonymous proposal contribution that was identified by AI, which recognized his intellectual habits, ending a two-week public challenge. The AI's ability to identify Buterin's contribution highlights its potential in analyzing and understanding human behavior. This development has implications for the Ethereum and broader AI communities.

title: Bitcoin can fall below $58K if one of its 'cleanest' metrics copies history: Analysis
content: Bitcoin NUPL metric data suggested that BTC price action should make new cycle lows in order to preserve historical patterns.
summary: Bitcoin's NUPL metric data suggests that the BTC price may fall below $58K to maintain historical patterns, potentially making new cycle lows. This analysis is based on historical patterns and the presumption that the metric will repeat itself. The price of Bitcoin may decline to preserve consistency with past trends.

title: Digital Chamber amicus brief urges dismissal of NY lawsuit over 39,069 Bitcoin wallets
content: The Digital Chamber filed an amicus brief urging the dismissal of the New York lawsuit seeking ownership of 39,069 dormant Bitcoin wallets, arguing it would set a dangerous precedent for self-custodial wallets.
summary: The Digital Chamber has filed an amicus brief to dismiss a New York lawsuit seeking ownership of 39,069 dormant Bitcoin wallets, citing concerns over setting a precedent for self-custodial wallets. The lawsuit's outcome could impact the cryptocurrency community. The brief argues for the protection of self-custodial wallets.

title: Trader loses $2M in ‘same-block backrun extraction’ exploit
content: One crypto trader noted the $2 million loss could have been prevented had the victim read the transaction route before signing the transaction.
summary: A crypto trader lost $2 million in a same-block backrun extraction exploit, which could have been prevented by reviewing the transaction route before signing. The loss highlights the importance of caution when executing transactions. The incident serves as a reminder for traders to be vigilant.

title: L1s face decentralization 'tug-of-war' as adoption grows: Injective CEO
content: Blockchains will face increasing pressure to compromise on decentralization to meet user demand for speed and scalability, Injective CEO Eric Chen said.
summary: Injective CEO Eric Chen states that blockchains will face pressure to compromise on decentralization as user demand for speed and scalability grows. This is due to the increasing adoption of blockchains, which may lead to a trade-off between decentralization and performance. Chen's comments highlight the challenges of balancing these competing interests.

title: US Bitcoin reserve hits snag as federal agencies debate for control: Bloomberg
content: The report follows comments from White House crypto adviser Patrick Witt in May, who said the administration was examining the legal implications of creating a Bitcoin reserve.
summary: A Bloomberg report indicates that the creation of a US Bitcoin reserve is encountering difficulties as various federal agencies are debating for control over the initiative. This development follows earlier statements from White House crypto adviser Patrick Witt in May, who mentioned the administration was investigating the legal ramifications of establishing such a reserve.

title: Strategy sells $216M Bitcoin, Bollinger bullish on BTC: Hodler's Digest, June 29-July 6, 2026
content: Bollinger Bands creator John Bollinger says the charts suggest Bitcoin could take off from here. Vitalik Buterin unveils a new Ethereum strawmap, but why is it taking so long?
summary: Bollinger Bands creator John Bollinger believes Bitcoin's charts indicate a potential uptrend, while Vitalik Buterin has unveiled a new Ethereum strawmap, sparking discussion on its development pace. This news combines insights from both cryptocurrency and blockchain development. The strawmap's release has been highly anticipated, given Ethereum's significant role in the crypto space.

title: BonkDAO reports $20M theft from ‘malicious governance proposal’
content: The developers behind the memecoin project said they had informed law enforcement after the attack and were working to “recover funds and identify those responsible.”
summary: BonkDAO, a memecoin project, has reported a theft of $20M due to a malicious governance proposal, and the developers are working with law enforcement to recover funds and identify those responsible. The attack has resulted in significant financial loss for the project. An investigation is ongoing to determine the perpetrators and prevent future incidents.

title: Ripple secures full MiCA license for crypto services across Europe
content: The Luxembourg approval completes the company's MiCA licensing process, enabling it to provide regulated crypto-asset services across the European Economic Area.
summary: Ripple has secured a full MiCA license, allowing it to offer regulated crypto-asset services across the European Economic Area. This license was granted by Luxembourg, completing Ripple's MiCA licensing process. The move expands Ripple's services in Europe.

title: UN agency moves Stellar blockchain payment initiative beyond pilot stage
content: The UN Development Programme said blockchain payment pilots in five countries cut costs, improved resilience and will now inform wider use across its humanitarian and development programs.
summary: The UN Development Programme has moved its Stellar blockchain payment initiative beyond the pilot stage after successful trials in five countries, which reduced costs and improved resilience. This initiative will now inform wider use across the programme's humanitarian and development programs. The success of the pilots is expected to lead to increased adoption of blockchain technology in the programme's operations.

title: TeraWulf shares rise after $19B Anthropic AI lease, JV sale
content: The Bitcoin miner signed a 20-year AI infrastructure lease with Anthropic and sold its majority stake in a separate AI data center joint venture.
summary: TeraWulf, a Bitcoin miner, has signed a 20-year lease with Anthropic for AI infrastructure and sold its majority stake in an AI data center joint venture, causing its shares to rise. This partnership aims to advance AI capabilities. The deal is valued at $19B.

title: Ondo Finance says tokenized stocks can now be used as collateral for perp trading
content: Last year, the tokenized real-world asset issuer launched 24/7 onchain access to more than 100 U.S. stocks and ETFs.
summary: Ondo Finance now allows tokenized stocks to be used as collateral for perpetual trading, building on its previous launch of 24/7 on-chain access to over 100 US stocks and ETFs. This move expands the use cases for tokenized assets. The development has implications for both crypto and traditional finance.

title: SEC plans crypto rule changes for exchanges and broker dealers in 2026 regulatory agenda
content: The SEC is laying the groundwork for cryptocurrency rule changes before the end of the year, with a blueprint for exchanges and brokers.
summary: The SEC plans to introduce cryptocurrency rule changes for exchanges and broker dealers in 2026. The regulatory agenda aims to lay the groundwork for these changes before the end of the year. This move may impact the cryptocurrency industry

title: 1kx, Blockchain Capital back $7.5 million KOR Protocol Series A at $100 million valuation
content: KOR Protocol is building an onchain clearinghouse that helps register and route creative works and pay out rights holders.
summary: KOR Protocol has raised $7.5 million in a Series A funding round at a $100 million valuation, backed by 1kx and Blockchain Capital, to develop an on-chain clearinghouse for registering and routing creative works. The protocol aims to facilitate payouts to rights holders. The investment highlights the growing interest in blockchain-based solutions for content ownership and monetization.

title: Tether puts $20 million behind Mercado Bitcoin amid Latin America’s tokenization boom
content: Mercado Bitcoin has grown from an early crypto exchange into a regulated digital financial services platform.
summary: Tether has invested $20 million in Mercado Bitcoin, a digital financial services platform in Latin America, amid a surge in tokenization in the region. Mercado Bitcoin has evolved from a crypto exchange to a regulated platform. This investment reflects the growing significance of Latin America in the global crypto market.

title: Gemini offers 0% stock trading in US as it looks to become ‘all-in-one financial super app’
content: Gemini said it is looking to become an “all-in-one financial super app" as it launches 0% commission stock trading for eligible U.S. users.
summary: Gemini has launched 0% commission stock trading for eligible US users, aiming to become an all-in-one financial super app. This move expands Gemini's services beyond cryptocurrency. The company seeks to provide a comprehensive financial platform.

title: Multi-trillion-dollar asset manager Vanguard hiring crypto exec to help influence market
content: The firm's head of digital assets will lead Vanguard's crypto-related strategy for personal wealth clients and interact with regulators.
summary: Vanguard, a multi-trillion-dollar asset manager, is hiring a crypto executive to lead its digital assets strategy for personal wealth clients and engage with regulators. This move indicates Vanguard's growing interest in the cryptocurrency market. The new hire will help shape the company's crypto-related strategy.

title: Bernstein maintains $36 TeraWulf target after $19 billion, 20-year Anthropic lease
content: Bernstein held its $36 TeraWulf price target after the miner's $19 billion Anthropic lease and $530 million sale of its Abernathy JV stake.
summary: Bernstein has maintained its $36 price target for TeraWulf after the company signed a 20-year lease with Anthropic worth $19 billion and sold its stake in Abernathy JV for $530 million. This development is significant for TeraWulf's future prospects. The lease and sale are expected to have a major impact on the company's operations.

title: Bitcoin’s early July bounce rides thin summer liquidity as half of supply still sits underwater: analysts
content: BTC traded around $63,500 after a six-day rally as Wintermute and Bitfinex flagged weak ETF demand, while K33 said 50% of supply is at a loss.
summary: Bitcoin's price has risen after a six-day rally, reaching around $63,500, despite analysts flagging weak ETF demand and noting that half of the supply is still at a loss. This price movement is occurring during a period of thin summer liquidity. The rally may be fragile due to these underlying conditions.

title: Coinbase secures UK investment services license to add derivatives and equities trading
content: Coinbase secured a UK investment services license to offer derivatives and equities trading in addition to crypto.
summary: Coinbase has obtained a UK investment services license, allowing the company to expand its services to include derivatives and equities trading, in addition to its existing cryptocurrency offerings. This move is expected to increase Coinbase's presence in the UK market. The license enables Coinbase to offer a broader range of financial products to its customers.

title: Galaxy delivers 133 MW of critical IT load to CoreWeave as Helios bitcoin mine turns AI hub
content: Galaxy delivered 133 MW of critical IT load to CoreWeave at its Helios campus, completing Phase I of the West Texas site's AI conversion.
summary: Galaxy has delivered 133 MW of critical IT load to CoreWeave, completing Phase I of the Helios campus's AI conversion in West Texas. The Helios site was initially a bitcoin mine but is being converted into an AI hub. This conversion marks a significant shift in the site's purpose.

title: Upbit, Naver stock-swap deal faces second delay amid proposed crypto law debate
content: The companies said that the development of the country's landmark crypto legislation could affect the deal's progress or outcome.
summary: Upbit and Naver's stock-swap deal is facing a second delay due to the ongoing debate over proposed crypto legislation in the country. The development of the landmark crypto law could impact the deal's progress or outcome. The companies are waiting for clarity on the legislation before proceeding.

title: What Is the U.S. Strategic Bitcoin Reserve?
content: The U.S. Strategic Bitcoin Reserve is a government-held stockpile of bitcoin, established by executive order in March 2025. The order treats Bitcoin as a long-term reserve asset, comparable to how the country holds gold, and directs that the coins placed in the reserve are not to be sold. Almost all of the U.S. government’s BTC […]
summary: The US government has established a Strategic Bitcoin Reserve, a stockpile of bitcoin held by the government, by executive order in March 2025. The reserve treats bitcoin as a long-term reserve asset, similar to gold, and the coins in the reserve are not to be sold. This move signifies a significant shift in the US government's stance on bitcoin.

title: Grayscale says Strategy’s recent bitcoin sales should restore confidence in its financing structure
content: "Strategy is selling more bitcoin. But this will restore confidence in its financing structure and help bitcoin find a more durable bottom."
summary: Grayscale's Strategy is selling bitcoin to restore confidence in its financing structure, which is expected to help bitcoin find a more stable bottom. This move aims to alleviate concerns and promote a more durable market. The sales are seen as a strategic decision to strengthen the company's financial foundation.

title: President Trump’s strategic bitcoin reserve hits a legal and jurisdictional snag: Bloomberg
content: President Trump's plans for a strategic bitcoin reserve are hitting a legal and jurisdictional snag, according to reporting from Bloomberg.
summary: President Trump's plans for a strategic bitcoin reserve are facing legal and jurisdictional issues. The plans are reportedly hitting a snag, according to Bloomberg. This development may impact the adoption of bitcoin

title: Paradigm leads $5.5 million seed round in M1X Global to expand tokenized sovereign debt platform
content: M1X Global, which worked with the Republic of the Marshall Islands on the issuance of the onchain sovereign bond USDM1, has raised funds.
summary: M1X Global has raised $5.5 million in a seed round led by Paradigm to expand its tokenized sovereign debt platform, which has previously worked with the Republic of the Marshall Islands on the issuance of the onchain sovereign bond USDM1. This funding will help the company grow its platform. The platform aims to bring sovereign debt to the blockchain.

title: BonkDAO loses $20 million following ‘malicious governance proposal’ attack
content: The funds have been tracked to crypto exchanges, and South Korea-based Upbit said it has suspended BONK deposits and withdrawals.
summary: BonkDAO has lost $20 million due to a malicious governance proposal attack, with the stolen funds tracked to various crypto exchanges. Upbit, a South Korea-based exchange, has suspended BONK deposits and withdrawals in response. The attack highlights the ongoing security risks in the crypto space.

title: Judge revives fraud claim against Barry Silbert, DCG in Genesis Yield lawsuit
content: The class action centers on Genesis Yield, a lending program that let customers deposit crypto in exchange for interest payments.
summary: A class action lawsuit against Barry Silbert and DCG has been revived, focusing on the Genesis Yield lending program, which allowed customers to deposit cryptocurrency in exchange for interest payments. The lawsuit alleges fraud and seeks damages. The revival of the claim may have implications for the crypto lending industry.

title: Vitalik Buterin proposes ‘Extremely Lean’ Ethereum, shrinking the chain to near-zero state with ZK proofs
content: Buterin outlined a two-step plan to shift Ethereum's state management to validators and replace per-epoch balance updates with ZK proofs.
summary: Vitalik Buterin has proposed a two-step plan to optimize Ethereum's state management by shifting it to validators and utilizing ZK proofs, aiming to shrink the chain to near-zero state. This plan would replace per-epoch balance updates, increasing efficiency. The proposal is part of an 'Extremely Lean' Ethereum concept.

title: SpaceX Nasdaq-100 Entry Brings Bitcoin Exposure to Passive Index Investors
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

SpaceX Nasdaq-100 Entry Brings Bitcoin Exposure to Passive Index Investors
SpaceX joins the Nasdaq-100 today, triggering ~$4.3B in passive inflows to a company holding 18,712 BTC. This creates rules-based institutional demand for Bitcoin through corporate balance sheets.
This post SpaceX Nasdaq-100 Entry Brings Bitcoin Exposure to Passive Index Investors first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Nick Ward.
summary: SpaceX has joined the Nasdaq-100, resulting in approximately $4.3 billion in passive inflows to the company, which holds 18,712 BTC, creating institutional demand for Bitcoin. This move brings Bitcoin exposure to passive index investors. The entry is expected to have a significant impact on Bitcoin's market.

title: Tether Invests $20 Million in Brazil’s Mercado Bitcoin
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Tether Invests $20 Million in Brazil’s Mercado Bitcoin
Tether invested $20 million in Brazil's Mercado Bitcoin to expand regulated blockchain finance, tokenization and stablecoin infrastructure across Latin America.
This post Tether Invests $20 Million in Brazil’s Mercado Bitcoin first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Tether invested $20 million in Brazil's Mercado Bitcoin to expand regulated blockchain finance and stablecoin infrastructure across Latin America. This investment aims to promote tokenization and stablecoin adoption in the region. The move is expected to boost the growth of the Latin American crypto market.

title: U.S. Bitcoin Reserve Stalls as Treasury and Commerce Vie for Control: Report
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

U.S. Bitcoin Reserve Stalls as Treasury and Commerce Vie for Control: Report
A dispute between the Treasury and Commerce departments over who should oversee the proposed U.S. Strategic Bitcoin Reserve has delayed its rollout more than a year after President Trump ordered its creation, according to a Bloomberg report.
This post U.S. Bitcoin Reserve Stalls as Treasury and Commerce Vie for Control: Report first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: A dispute between the US Treasury and Commerce departments has delayed the rollout of the proposed US Strategic Bitcoin Reserve, which was ordered by President Trump over a year ago. The delay is due to a disagreement over who should oversee the reserve. The rollout has been stalled for over a year.

title: Bitcoin Suisse Wins Abu Dhabi License, Extends Digital Asset Push into the UAE
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Bitcoin Suisse Wins Abu Dhabi License, Extends Digital Asset Push into the UAE
Bitcoin Suisse won regulatory approval in Abu Dhabi, allowing it to offer institutional crypto services across the UAE.
This post Bitcoin Suisse Wins Abu Dhabi License, Extends Digital Asset Push into the UAE first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Bitcoin Suisse has received regulatory approval in Abu Dhabi, enabling the company to provide institutional crypto services across the UAE. This move marks an extension of Bitcoin Suisse's digital asset push into the region. The approval allows the company to expand its services in the UAE.

title: USDT Returns to Bitcoin: RGB and UTEXO Enable Private Lightning Settlements
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

USDT Returns to Bitcoin: RGB and UTEXO Enable Private Lightning Settlements
After years of development, UTEXO—backed by Tether—rolls out Bitcoin-native USDT with client-side validation, slashing fees and middlemen compared to Tron or Ethereum rails.
This post USDT Returns to Bitcoin: RGB and UTEXO Enable Private Lightning Settlements first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Juan Galt.
summary: USDT has returned to Bitcoin through UTEXO, a platform backed by Tether, enabling private Lightning settlements with lower fees and fewer middlemen. This development allows for Bitcoin-native USDT with client-side validation. The move slashes fees compared to using Tron or Ethereum rails.

title: President Trump Signals Openness to Bitcoin in Trump Accounts, Calls Himself ‘a Big Fan of Crypto’
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

President Trump Signals Openness to Bitcoin in Trump Accounts, Calls Himself ‘a Big Fan of Crypto’
President Trump said Bitcoin could eventually be included in the new Trump Accounts savings program, calling himself "a big fan of crypto."
This post President Trump Signals Openness to Bitcoin in Trump Accounts, Calls Himself ‘a Big Fan of Crypto’ first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: President Trump expressed openness to including Bitcoin in the Trump Accounts savings program, calling himself a big fan of crypto. This move could potentially increase Bitcoin's mainstream adoption. The statement was made in relation to the new Trump Accounts savings program.

title: Russian-Sberbank Plans Crypto Wallet and Digital Depository by December
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Russian-Sberbank Plans Crypto Wallet and Digital Depository by December
Russia's biggest bank, Sberbank, plans to launch a Bitcoin and crypto wallet plus digital asset custody by December as the country rolls out its new crypto framework.

summary: Sberbank, Russia's largest bank, plans to launch a crypto wallet and digital depository by December, as part of the country's new crypto framework. The wallet will support Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies. This move is a significant step in Russia's adoption of cryptocurrencies.

content:
Bitcoin Magazine

title: The Download: your stake in OpenAI, and the Treasury’s AI warning

title: Savi’s app aims to protect consumers from realistic AI scams like kidnappers demanding ransom
content: The company just raised $7 million in seed funding, and is launching its app for iPhone and Android on Tuesday.
summary: Savi's app aims to protect consumers from AI-powered scams, such as kidnappers demanding ransom, and has raised $7 million in seed funding. The app will be available on iPhone and Android. This development is significant for consumers' safety against AI scams.

title: The first American autonomous ground vehicles are fighting in Ukraine
content: Forterra has deployed more than 100 of its self-driving ATVs in conflict zones in Ukraine.
summary: Forterra has deployed over 100 autonomous ground vehicles in Ukraine, marking the first time American autonomous vehicles are used in combat. These vehicles are being used to support Ukrainian forces. The deployment showcases the increasing use of AI in warfare.

title: The ‘first’ AI-run ransomware attack still needed a human
content: An AI agent carried out the technical execution of a real-world ransomware attack for the first known time, but new details show a human still chose the victim, set up the infrastructure, and supplied stolen credentials — meaning it wasn't quite the fully autonomous cybercrime debut that last week's headlines suggested.
summary: A recent ransomware attack was carried out with the help of an AI agent, but it still required human intervention to choose the victim and set up the infrastructure. The AI agent was used for the technical execution of the attack. This incident marks the first known time an AI has been used in a real-world ransomware attack.

title: US investors will soon get access to SK Hynix, another memory maker riding the AI boom
content: SK Hynix is experiencing a boom credited to AI. It will ride that to a multibillion-dollar U.S. IPO, expected to take place on Friday.
summary: SK Hynix, a memory maker, is experiencing a boom due to AI and will soon have a multibillion-dollar US IPO. The IPO is expected to take place on Friday. This move will give US investors access to the company.

title: You can now customize Siri’s pace and expressivity in the latest iOS 27 beta
content: The update is part of Apple's broader effort to make Siri feel more natural and personal, as it rebuilds the assistant around generative AI.
summary: Apple's latest iOS 27 beta update allows users to customize Siri's pace and expressivity, aiming to make the assistant feel more natural and personal. This update is part of Apple's effort to rebuild Siri around generative AI. The goal is to enhance the user experience with a more personalized and natural-feeling assistant.

title: Every major tech layoff in 2026 that has name-checked AI
content: A running look — in reverse chronological order — at the bigger tech companies that have announced significant layoffs this year with AI as a stated factor.
summary: Several major tech companies have announced significant layoffs in 2026, citing AI as a contributing factor. This article provides a running list of these companies, focusing on the role of AI in their layoff decisions. The list is presented in reverse chronological order.

title: If you use Google, you’re training its AI. Here’s how to opt out.
content: Consider this a belated PSA: A recent change to Google’s privacy settings is allowing the company to store more of your data, including media such as “images, files, and audio and video recordings,” to improve its AI models.
summary: Google's recent change to its privacy settings allows the company to store more user data to improve its AI models, including images, files, and audio and video recordings. Users can opt out of this data collection. This change affects users who utilize Google's services.

title: Reddit is using LLMs to solve a problem LLMs largely created
content: In the AI era, platforms have no choice but to fight fire with fire to cull spam.
summary: Reddit is utilizing Large Language Models (LLMs) to combat spam, a problem that LLMs have largely contributed to. This approach aims to mitigate the issue by using the same technology that created it. The move highlights the evolving relationship between AI and online platforms.

title: Midjourney wants Hollywood studios to reveal the details of their AI usage
content: As part of an ongoing legal dispute with three Hollywood studios, Midjourney is seeking to compel those studios to reveal how they use AI themselves.
summary: Midjourney, an AI image generator, is demanding that three Hollywood studios disclose their own AI usage as part of an ongoing legal dispute. This move aims to shed light on how major entertainment companies are implementing AI in their operations amidst broader legal challenges.

title: Anthropic wants to develop its own drugs
content:
At the event "The Briefing: AI for Science" earlier this week, Anthropic announced Claude Science, a new "AI workbench for scientists" that pulls fragmented tools and datasets into one environment, and generates figures and visuals. Anthropic, already dominating the industry with its popular coding tools and powerful AI models, framed the launch around what it says is AI's potential to "dramatically accelerate the pace of scientific discovery and the development of healthcare interventions," and touted a long list of biotech and pharma customers already using Claude. 
Anthropic also went a step further, saying it would develop drugs of its  …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Anthropic unveiled Claude Science, an AI workbench that consolidates tools and datasets for scientists, aiming to speed up scientific discovery and drug development, and announced plans to develop its own pharmaceuticals using AI.

title: OpenAI floats giving Trump administration 5 percent cut of AI boom
content:
OpenAI has floated giving the US government a 5 percent ownership stake as a way of easing tensions with the Trump administration and blunting mounting public backlash against AI, according to the Financial Times.
CEO Sam Altman argued that giving the public a financial interest in the company would be the best way to share the upside of AI, the FT reported, citing two unnamed people familiar with the talks. He's said to have first pitched the idea to Trump early last year.
Altman reportedly suggested the 5 percent figure. Based on OpenAI's latest funding round, which ended with the company valued at $852 billion, that stake would be worth …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: OpenAI has reportedly discussed offering the U.S. government a 5% ownership stake, valued at around $42 billion, to ease tensions with the Trump administration and address public concerns about AI. CEO Sam Altman is said to have pitched the idea to Trump, suggesting a financial share of the AI boom would align public interests.

title: Facing US export controls, China's DeepSeek plans to make its own chips
content: It's early, but the plan is to reduce dependency on Nvidia and Huawei.
summary: China's DeepSeek is planning to develop its own chips to reduce dependence on Nvidia and Huawei due to US export controls. This move is still in its early stages. The goal is to decrease reliance on foreign technology.

title: How AI could enable autonomous robot workers in workplaces—and maybe homes
content: Top robotics researchers and founders explain how robot autonomy is evolving.
summary: Top robotics researchers and founders discuss the evolution of robot autonomy, enabled by AI, which could lead to autonomous robot workers in workplaces and homes. This development could significantly impact various industries and daily life. AI is playing a crucial role in this advancement.

title: Secret Claude tracker shocks users after Anthropic’s anti-surveillance stance
content: Anthropic accused of spying on users; engineer says “experiment” is over.
summary: Anthropic, a company known for its AI technology, has been accused of spying on users through a secret tracker in its Claude tool, contradicting its anti-surveillance stance. An engineer stated that the experiment is now over. This incident raises concerns about user privacy and trust in AI companies.

title: UK regulator warns of "arms race" to keep up with AI use in financial services
content: FCA official makes case for greater powers for watchdog as millions use technology for personal finance decisions.
summary: A UK regulator has warned of an 'arms race' to keep up with the increasing use of AI in financial services, with millions using the technology for personal finance decisions. The regulator is calling for greater powers to oversee the industry. This warning highlights the growing importance of AI in finance.

title: After spooking Trump into safety testing, Anthropic AI models get global release
content: US lifts curbs on Anthropic’s advanced Fable and Mythos models.
summary: The US has lifted restrictions on Anthropic's advanced AI models, Fable and Mythos, permitting their global release. This follows earlier safety testing prompted by concerns raised by former President Trump.

title: South Korea to spend $1T on more memory chip production and humanoid robots
content: South Korea targets physical AI lead and commercial humanoid robots by 2028.
summary: South Korea plans to invest $1 trillion in expanding memory chip manufacturing and developing commercial humanoid robots, aiming to become a global leader in physical AI by 2028.

title: Anthropic says Alibaba must be punished for largest Claude cloning attack
content: Alibaba allegedly used 25,000 accounts to mine Claude over 28.8 million exchanges.
summary: Anthropic claims Alibaba used about 25,000 accounts to repeatedly query its Claude model, resulting in roughly 28.8 million exchanges, effectively cloning the AI. The company is calling for punitive action against Alibaba for the largest known Claude‑cloning attack.

title: OpenAI and Broadcom announce chip designed for LLM inference at scale
content: The silicon race is heating up amid the struggle to keep up with demand.
summary: OpenAI and Broadcom have announced a new chip specifically built for large language model inference at scale, aiming to meet the surging demand for AI compute power. The announcement underscores the intensifying silicon race as companies race to provide hardware capable of handling massive AI workloads.

title: SpaceX IPO powers record $3.86 billion in tokenized equities trading in June
content: SpaceX tokens captured $1.19 billion, or 31% of total monthly volume, with Backpack’s SPCX leading trading at $1.08 billion.
summary: Tokenized equities trading reached a record $3.86 billion in June, largely driven by SpaceX tokens which accounted for $1.19 billion (31%) of the total volume. Backpack's SPCX token was a significant contributor, leading trading with $1.08 billion.

title: Former Tether investment chief is looking to sell part of his stake in the stablecoin giant: Bloomberg
content: Richard Heathcote is reportedly working with PJT Partners to find a buyer for a small part of his 1.26% holding in the issuer of USDT following his transition to an advisory role.
summary: Richard Heathcote, the former investment chief at Tether, is reportedly seeking to sell a small portion of his 1.26% stake in the stablecoin giant. He is working with PJT Partners to find a buyer for his holdings following his transition to an advisory role.

title: Bitcoin's recent macro relief faces a challenge from Japanese interest rates
content: Japanese bond yields continue to rise, lifting their U.S. counterparts and posing a potential headwind for risk assets.
summary: Rising Japanese bond yields are influencing U.S. bond yields, which could create a challenging environment for risk assets, potentially impacting Bitcoin's recent macro relief. This suggests a potential headwind for the cryptocurrency market due to global economic shifts.

title: Securitize eyes acquisitions with $400 million war chest after going public, CEO says
content: Following its NYSE debut, Securitize CEO Carlos Domingo said the firm wants to expand its institutional tokenization platform rather than buy competitors.
summary: Following its debut on the NYSE, Securitize, a company focused on institutional tokenization, has secured a $400 million war chest for acquisitions. CEO Carlos Domingo stated the funds would be used to expand their platform, not to acquire competitors, signaling a growth strategy in the blockchain and digital asset space.

title: Nigel Farage to resign from UK Parliament amid crypto “gift” scandal, will stand in by-election
content: The Reform party leader and Brexit champion confirmed that he was the subject of probes by UK authorities following reports of what he called “gifts” from figures tied to cry pto ventures.
summary: Nigel Farage, leader of the Reform party, is resigning from UK Parliament after being investigated for "gifts" received from figures linked to crypto ventures. He will contest a by-election, with the scandal highlighting the intersection of high-profile politics and the cryptocurrency industry.

title: Institutional crypto exchange EDX lands $76M from SBI Holdings
content: The funding round comes as institutional investors continue backing crypto market infrastructure despite slower venture investment across the digital asset sector.
summary: Institutional crypto exchange EDX has successfully raised $76 million from SBI Holdings. This significant funding round highlights ongoing institutional confidence and investment in crypto market infrastructure, even amidst a broader slowdown in venture capital across the digital asset sector.

title: Bitcoin bulls shake off Strategy's $216M BTC sale as price overtakes $64K
content: Bitcoin reversed course from its Monday open selloff and closed the day above $64,000 as bulls began to price in Strategy’s selling.
summary: Bitcoin's price recovered from a Monday selloff, closing above $64,000 despite a significant $216 million BTC sale by Strategy. Bulls quickly priced in the institutional selling, leading to a reversal in the cryptocurrency's market course.

title: The fanfiction community is at war with AI — and itself
content:
Fanfiction communities are trying to hunt down writers who haven’t written works with their own hands. | Image: Álvaro Bernis / The Verge	

Over the past week, a new fanworks movement has kicked off, with the aim to root out authors using generative AI. But the detection methods being implemented are questionable, and any fanfic writer could be caught in the crossfire.
Broad distaste around the use of Claude, ChatGPT, and other AI tools has long been a thing in creative communities, including the world of fanfiction. Readers and writers have passed around tips for spotting supposedly AI-generated works, citing anything from em dashes to the broad concept of purple prose. But on June 29th, an anonymous X account called @heatedrivalryai promised a seemingly more reliable solution …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: The fanfiction community has launched a movement to identify and remove works created using generative AI tools like Claude and ChatGPT. This effort stems from a broad dislike of AI-generated content, but the detection methods being used are questionable and risk unfairly targeting human writers. An anonymous X account has emerged, promising a more reliable solution to this conflict within the community.

title: Trump gets OpenAI to offer US 5% stake, far lower than Sanders’ target
content: Insiders say Sam Altman is in active talks with the Trump administration.
summary: Insiders report that Sam Altman is in active talks with the Trump administration about OpenAI offering a 5% stake in the United States, a figure that is much lower than the target suggested by Senator Sanders. The move highlights potential political involvement and investment discussions surrounding the AI firm.

title: Google’s AI buildout drove 37% increase in electricity use in 2025
content: Google tries balancing AI data center emissions with clean energy efforts.
summary: Google's rapid expansion of AI workloads is projected to raise its electricity consumption by 37% in 2025. The company is attempting to offset the resulting emissions by increasing its use of clean energy sources in its data centers.

title: New attack provides one more reason why AI browsers are a bad idea
content: Telling an LLM that 2 + 2 = 5 is enough to make it follow forbidden instructions.
summary: Researchers have demonstrated a new jailbreak attack where simply convincing a large language model that 2 + 2 = 5 causes it to execute prohibited instructions, highlighting another drawback of AI-driven browsers.

title: NYT slams Microsoft for building copyright-infringing supercomputer for OpenAI
content: NYT shifts OpenAI/Microsoft copyright claims after SCOTUS ruling against Sony.
summary: The New York Times criticizes Microsoft for creating a supercomputer for OpenAI that it says infringes copyright, following a Supreme Court decision against Sony. The article highlights concerns over the legality of the hardware used to train large AI models.

title: Notion killing Skiff-influenced email app since most users use AI agents instead
content: Notion is "going all in on using agents to run your inbox."
summary: Notion is shutting down a Skiff-inspired email app, citing that most users now rely on AI agents to manage their inboxes, and will focus on integrating AI-driven agents for email handling.

title: IBM claims world’s first sub-1 nanometer chip technology
content: IBM’s nanostack transistors could boost chip performance or energy efficiency.
summary: IBM announced the development of the world’s first sub‑1 nanometer chip technology, using its nanostack transistor architecture. The new transistors are expected to significantly improve chip performance and energy efficiency, potentially accelerating compute‑intensive workloads.

title: Bitcoin stalls as open interest decline raises questions about rally's staying power
content: BTC retreated from a two-week high of $64,500 as falling open interest and weak spot demand cast doubt on the sustainability of July's 8.4% advance.
summary: Bitcoin retreated from a two-week high of $64,500, with falling open interest and weak spot demand casting doubt on the sustainability of its 8.4% rally in July. This decline raises questions about the staying power of recent gains for BTC.

title: Bitcoin drops after a run at $64,000, shrugging off Strategy's $213 million BTC sale
content: The token touched $64,400 overnight before easing back, still up about 6% on the week. A fresh missile strike on a Qatari gas ship in the Strait of Hormuz lifted oil and tested the late-June peace deal as Asian tech sold off again.
summary: Bitcoin's price briefly touched $64,400 before dropping, despite a significant BTC sale by Strategy, and is still up 6% on the week. The price movement occurred amidst global events such as a missile strike on a Qatari gas ship. The overall market saw Asian tech stocks sell off.

title: MiCA-compliant euro stablecoins grew 128% ahead of July 1 deadline, says Decta
content: Decta's report said the market cap of eight MiCA-compliant euro stablecoins rose to $673.9 million in the year before Europe’s CASP transition period ended.
summary: The market capitalization of MiCA-compliant euro stablecoins grew 128% to $673.9 million in the year leading up to the end of Europe's CASP transition period, according to a report by Decta. This growth indicates increasing adoption of stablecoins in the region. The report highlights the impact of regulatory compliance on the stablecoin market.

title: Yield Guild Games cuts 35 staff, shuts game publisher to focus on AI
content: Yield Guild Games said tough crypto and video game market conditions mean its YGG Play business is no longer commercially sustainable.
summary: Yield Guild Games is cutting 35 staff and shutting down its game publisher to focus on AI due to tough market conditions in crypto and video games. The decision is a result of its YGG Play business being no longer commercially sustainable. This shift in focus may indicate a new direction for the company.

title: Strategy’s Bitcoin sale may give BTC a ‘durable bottom,’ Grayscale says
content: “Investors are now more confident about the instrument,” said Grayscale’s Zach Pandl regarding STRC, which reclaimed $90 for the first time in three weeks.
summary: Grayscale's Zach Pandl comments on Strategy's Bitcoin sale, stating it may give BTC a durable bottom, with investors showing increased confidence, as STRC reaches $90 for the first time in three weeks. This development could be a positive sign for Bitcoin's market. The statement suggests a potential shift in investor sentiment.

title: Trump says he became ‘a big crypto guy’ partly for politics
content: US President Donald Trump once called Bitcoin “a scam,” but admitted on Monday that he got involved in crypto “a little bit for politics.”
summary: US President Donald Trump stated he became involved in cryptocurrency partly for political reasons, despite previously calling Bitcoin a scam. Trump's comments suggest a change in his stance on crypto. This change may be significant for the crypto community.

title: Former Tether CIO seeks to sell stake in stablecoin issuer, Bloomberg reports
content: The reported transaction comes as Tether maintains it has no plans to go public, even as other crypto companies pursue or delay IPOs.
summary: A former Tether CIO is looking to sell their stake in the stablecoin issuer, according to a report by Bloomberg. This news comes as Tether has stated it has no plans to go public, unlike some other crypto companies. The potential sale is a notable development in the cryptocurrency space.

title: Bitcoin recovers from Strategy's BTC sale, funding rates hit 9%: Are bulls back?
content: Bitcoin sold off as news of Strategy selling BTC shook investors’ nerve but the quick rebound suggests bulls remain ambitiously positioned.
summary: Bitcoin recently experienced a sell-off after news of Strategy selling BTC, but it quickly rebounded, suggesting that bulls are still confidently invested. The funding rates have hit 9%, indicating a positive trend. This rebound may signify a continued upward trajectory for Bitcoin.

title: Bitmine announces $74M Ether buys as chair says ‘greater chances of Clarity Act passage’
content: Bitmine's big buy contrasts with crypto treasury peer Strategy, which reported selling millions of dollars worth of its Bitcoin holdings on Monday.
summary: Bitmine has announced a $74M purchase of Ether, contrasting with another crypto treasury company Strategy, which sold millions of dollars worth of Bitcoin holdings. This move comes as the company's chair expresses optimism about the passage of the Clarity Act. The purchase indicates a significant investment in cryptocurrency.

title: Tether’s former CIO seeks buyers for part of his stake in the firm: Bloomberg
content: Former Tether CIO Richard Heathcote is seeking buyers for part of his 1.26% stake in the firm, according to Bloomberg.
summary: Richard Heathcote, Tether's former CIO, is looking to sell part of his 1.26% stake in the company. This move may indicate a shift in his involvement with the firm. The sale is reported by Bloomberg.

title: Two traders sue Polymarket over disputed resolution of Strategy bitcoin sale market
content: Plaintiffs allege Polymarket wrongly resolved the market as "No" despite Strategy's SEC filing disclosing it sold 32 BTC between May 26 and May 31.
summary: Two traders are suing Polymarket over a disputed resolution of a bitcoin sale market related to Strategy, alleging the platform wrongly resolved the market as 'No' despite a SEC filing showing the sale of 32 BTC. The plaintiffs claim Polymarket's resolution was incorrect. The case involves cryptocurrency and a decentralized market platform.

title: Trump-Backed American Bitcoin (ABTC) Pushes Treasury Past 8,000 BTC
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Trump-Backed American Bitcoin (ABTC) Pushes Treasury Past 8,000 BTC
American Bitcoin (Nasdaq: ABTC) said its treasury has surpassed 8,000 BTC.
This post Trump-Backed American Bitcoin (ABTC) Pushes Treasury Past 8,000 BTC first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: American Bitcoin's treasury has surpassed 8,000 BTC, a milestone for the Trump-backed company. This increase is a significant addition to the company's holdings. The company announced this achievement, marking a new high for its treasury.

title: Strive (ASST) Adds 17.76 Bitcoin as Falling Prices Boost Its Quarterly Yield
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Strive (ASST) Adds 17.76 Bitcoin as Falling Prices Boost Its Quarterly Yield
Strive (ASST) added a symbolic 17.76 BTC, pushing its treasury to 19,882 bitcoin as a sharp quarterly accumulation spree delivered a 24% BTC yield.
This post Strive (ASST) Adds 17.76 Bitcoin as Falling Prices Boost Its Quarterly Yield first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Strive (ASST) has added 17.76 bitcoin to its treasury, bringing the total to 19,882 bitcoin, due to a sharp quarterly accumulation that resulted in a 24% BTC yield. This increase is attributed to falling bitcoin prices. The addition is a notable event for Strive's BTC holdings.

title: Claude Cowork expands to mobile and web
content: With this update, users can start a task from their desk, get status updates on their phone, and pick up the finished output later — even if their laptop is closed.
summary: Claude Cowork has expanded its services to mobile and web, allowing users to start tasks on their desk, receive updates on their phone, and access finished outputs later. This update enables seamless workflow continuity across different devices. The expansion aims to enhance user experience and productivity.

title: Microsoft lays off nearly 5,000 employees across Xbox, commercial sales
content: Microsoft cut around 4,800 roles, or 2.1% of its global workforce, on Monday — the latest in a series of layoffs that’s stoking fears of AI replacing jobs. The layoffs will hit Xbox and commercial sales the hardest.
summary: Microsoft laid off nearly 5,000 employees, impacting Xbox and commercial sales, amid concerns that AI may replace jobs. The layoffs account for 2.1% of Microsoft's global workforce. This move is part of a larger trend of job cuts in the tech industry.

title: Station F ramps up as a launchpad for Europe’s hottest AI startups
content: Station F, a Paris-based startup hub founded by French billionaire Xavier Niel, is gearing up for a new edition of its F/ai accelerator program in a bid to strengthen its positioning as a stepping stone for promising AI startups.
summary: Station F, a Paris-based startup hub, is launching a new edition of its F/ai accelerator program to support promising AI startups, founded by French billionaire Xavier Niel. The program aims to strengthen Station F's position as a key launchpad for Europe's hottest AI startups. This move is expected to boost the growth of AI innovation in Europe.

title: Amazon will stop accepting new customers for Mechanical Turk
content: These may be the last days of Amazon’s Mechanical Turk.
summary: Amazon's Mechanical Turk, a platform for crowdsourced work, will stop accepting new customers, marking a potential end to the service. Mechanical Turk has been used for various tasks, including data labeling for AI models. The move may impact businesses and researchers relying on the platform.

title: New Google commercial imagines a Declaration of Independence written with help from AI
content: Two hundred and fifty years after the signing of the Declaration of Independence, a new commercial asks: What if the Founding Fathers had access to Google Workspace?
summary: A new Google commercial envisions the Founding Fathers using Google Workspace, powered by AI, to write the Declaration of Independence. The ad explores how AI could have assisted in the creation of this historic document. This concept highlights the potential of AI in enhancing human creativity and productivity.

title: Alibaba reportedly bans employees from using Claude Code
content: Alibaba has reportedly classified Claude Code as high-risk software.
summary: Alibaba has banned its employees from using Claude Code, classifying it as high-risk software. This decision may be due to security concerns. The specifics of the ban are not detailed in the report.

title: The only AI glossary you’ll need this year
content: The rise of AI has brought an avalanche of new terms and slang. Here is a glossary with definitions of some of the most important words and phrases you might encounter.
summary: The article provides an AI glossary with definitions of important terms and phrases related to artificial intelligence. This glossary aims to help individuals understand the new language surrounding AI. The rise of AI has introduced a multitude of new words and slang.

title: Mark Zuckerberg tells staff that AI agents haven’t progressed as quickly as he’d hoped
content: At an internal meeting, the Meta CEO reportedly said that AI development efforts were not moving as quickly as anticipated.
summary: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg told staff that the development of AI agents is lagging behind his expectations. He indicated that progress is slower than anticipated, suggesting challenges in achieving the company's AI goals.

title: Solos debuts an even lighter version of its camera-less smart glasses
content:
The AirGo A6 are available in multiple designs including several transparent color options. | Image: Solos	

Solos announced a new version of its AirGo smart glasses, one that forgoes cameras for a sleeker design and an AI assistant that relies on voice interactions. Last year's AirGo A5 weighed 36 to 40 grams depending on the frame style, but the new AirGo A6 weigh around 19 grams. Part of the weight savings comes from thinner temple arms housing speakers, batteries, and other electronics. For comparison, the new Meta Glasses announced last month weigh around 54 to nearly 60 grams depending on the style.
Pricing and availability for the new AirGo A6 hasn't been finalized yet, but the smart glasses will support "full prescription lens compatibilit …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Solos has debuted the AirGo A6, a new version of its smart glasses that weighs around 19 grams and features an AI assistant with voice interactions, forgoing cameras for a sleeker design. The AirGo A6 comes in multiple designs and color options, including transparent frames. Pricing and availability have not been finalized yet.

title: I spy
content:
I’m just doing my job, but turns out, it feels lousy holding other people’s privacy in your hands.	

I've long argued that Hollywood has simultaneously set and ruined our expectations for smart glasses. But after binge-watching two seasons of Netflix's A Man on the Inside, this is perhaps the first time I've seen Hollywood, perhaps inadvertently, illustrate the biggest cultural problem with smart glasses as they stand today. 
In a nutshell, Ted Danson plays Charles Nieuwendyk, an elderly widower who finds a new purpose working for a private investigator. Armed with a pair of Ray-Ban Meta-like glasses, a voice recorder, and a smartphone, Nieuwendyk infiltrates a retirement home, and several privacy-infringing hijinks ensue as he hunts for t …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: A Netflix show inadvertently highlights the cultural problem with smart glasses, particularly the invasion of privacy, as a character uses them for surveillance. The show's portrayal raises concerns about the impact of smart glasses on society. The article discusses the implications of this technology.

title: Microsoft is laying off 4,800 employees
content:
Satya Nadella in February 2026. | Photo by Sven Hoppe / picture alliance via Getty Images	

A year after cutting around 9,100 employees, Microsoft is making further layoffs today as it begins its new financial year. The software maker is laying off around 4,800 employees today, approximately 2.1 percent of its workforce. Most of the employees affected by today's cuts are in Microsoft's commercial sales business or the company's Xbox division.
In an internal memo to employees, Amy Coleman, executive vice president and Microsoft's chief people officer, blamed the job losses on a changing technology industry and the "need to adjust resources and roles and shift how we operate" to respond to how AI is impacting companies like Microsof …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Microsoft is laying off 4,800 employees, approximately 2.1% of its workforce, mainly in commercial sales and Xbox divisions, due to a changing tech industry and the impact of AI. The layoffs come a year after cutting 9,100 employees. Microsoft is adjusting resources to respond to AI's impact.

title: Some of the nation’s rich are letting AI teach their kids
content:
Most Americans don't trust AI. It's proven that it doesn't know what safe toppings for pizza are. People don't even want to listen to AI music. But none of that matters for some of America's wealthy, who are turning to AI to teach their kids instead of traditional schools.
Companies like Forge Prep and Alpha School are charging families tens of thousands of dollars to turn their kids into beta testers for AI tutors and "interactive project-based workshops." Unsurprisingly, Silicon Valley have been major adopters of this new model. Shaun Johnson, a San Francisco-based venture capitalist, told The Wall Street Journal that he plans to send his …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Wealthy American families are using AI to teach their kids, with companies like Forge Prep and Alpha School offering AI-powered tutoring and workshops for tens of thousands of dollars. This trend is particularly popular in Silicon Valley, with some venture capitalists embracing the new model. The move reflects a growing interest in AI-based education among the affluent.

title: Infuriating Google commercial imagines the founding fathers embracing AI
content:
I call BS: the founding fathers definitely would have been Microsoft Teams users. | Image: Google	

"Group project, but make it 1776." That's how a new commercial for Google Workspace opens. And things only get cringier from there. The clip imagines what it would be like if the founding fathers turned to Google's collaboration tools and Gemini to help them draft the Declaration of Independence. 
Ben Franklin texts Thomas Jefferson to check on the status of a draft, who takes a photo and uses AI to transcribe it into a Google Doc. Franklin and Adams hop in to make edits in suggestion mode, Gemini finds them a meeting time, takes notes during a Google Meet call, and then Nano Banana whips up a seal for the United States featuring a turkey ( …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: A new Google commercial imagines the founding fathers using Google Workspace and AI to draft the Declaration of Independence, with features like text transcription and virtual meetings. The ad showcases Google's collaboration tools in a humorous and anachronistic way. The commercial highlights the potential of AI and Google's tools to enhance productivity and teamwork.

title: A behind-the-scenes look at Midjourney’s medical scanner leaves many questions unanswered
content:
A scan of an imaging phantom, segmented to validate how cleanly structures separate under controlled conditions. | Image: Midjourney Medical	

Midjourney has shown more of its futuristic medical scanner. It still hasn't shown much proof it works. 
The AI startup, best known for generating images, released a behind-the-scenes video of its dunk-tank ultrasound scanner, which it plans to deploy in spas and hopes will transform medicine with cheap, detailed, radiation-free imaging. The nearly 20-minute tour comes from tech YouTuber Marcin Plaza, who also happens to be an engineer at the company.
Plaza frankly describes the scanner as scores of ultrasound probes "hacked apart and slapped on a glorified hot tub with an elevator in it," connected to off-the-shelf computers and Raspberry …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Midjourney, the AI image‑generation startup, released a behind‑the‑scenes video of its experimental ultrasound scanner, describing it as a collection of hacked‑apart probes attached to a hot‑tub‑like structure with off‑the‑shelf computers and Raspberry Pis. The company claims the device could provide cheap, radiation‑free imaging for spas, but the video shows little concrete evidence of its performance, prompting skepticism about its practicality.

title: Google built a great smart speaker, but Gemini isn’t ready for it
content:
The Google Home Speaker is Google’s first smart speaker in years. And it’s pretty! | Photo: Jennifer Patison Tuohy / The Verge	

Smart speakers have spent the past few years searching for a compelling second act. Beyond music, timers, and controlling your lights, they've struggled to justify taking up space on the kitchen counter. AI promised to change that.
Amazon debuted its new hardware powered by a revamped Alexa last fall, and now it's finally Google's turn. The Google Home Speaker is the company's first new smart speaker in six years and its first "built for Gemini." After years of neglect, Google appears to be finally getting serious about the smart home - again. The new speaker is the clearest sign yet. Gemini for Home, however, still feels unfinished.
Google …

Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Google has launched its first new smart speaker in six years, the Google Home Speaker, which is designed to integrate with its Gemini AI. However, the article argues that Gemini is still unfinished and not fully ready to showcase its capabilities on the device. The launch signals Google's renewed focus on the smart home market.

title: Musk’s X poses “serious risk to Americans’ privacy,” advocates warn FTC
content: FTC urged to reject Elon Musk’s bid to end X monitoring amid AI concerns.
summary: Advocates warn that Elon Musk's plan to stop monitoring content on X could pose a serious risk to the privacy of American users, especially given the platform's use of artificial intelligence for data analysis. They urge the FTC to reject the bid, emphasizing the need for stronger privacy protections.

title: Google's new Nano Banana 2 Lite image model is its fastest and cheapest yet
content: They may not look as good, but Nano Banana 2 Lite images only take a few seconds to create.
summary: Google has unveiled the Nano Banana 2 Lite, an image generation model that is the fastest and cheapest in its series. It can create images in just a few seconds, though the visual quality is lower than more advanced models.

title: Achieving operational excellence with AI
content: Frameworks like Lean Six Sigma and business process management (BPM) first gained traction because they promised clarity in the chaos—a structured way to bring order to messy, sprawling operations. Lean Six Sigma emphasized statistical rigor and quality control; BPM created end-to-end maps of how work should flow across departments. Both offered a repeatable way to…
summary: The article discusses how AI can be integrated with frameworks like Lean Six Sigma and business process management to bring order and improve efficiency in complex operations. It explains that AI enhances statistical rigor and end‑to‑end process mapping, leading to operational excellence.

title: Trump's plan to redesign every .gov website leads to AI-designed horrors
content: A year in, National Design Studio delays plan to update government web standards.
summary: The National Design Studio has delayed its plan to overhaul .gov website standards originally proposed under Trump, citing challenges that have resulted in AI-generated design proposals that are considered problematic. The delay highlights concerns over the use of AI in public web design.

title: South Korea plans to train entire military as "drone warriors"
content: Half-million-strong military will train on drones as “universal combat tool.”
summary: South Korea announced that its 500,000-strong armed forces will be trained to operate drones as a "universal combat tool." The plan aims to make every soldier a "drone warrior," integrating unmanned aerial systems across all branches of the military.

title: Google finally releases a Finance Android app, promises iOS version later in 2026
content: It took 20 years, but the Finance app arrives just in time to be packed full of AI.
summary: Google has finally launched its long‑awaited Finance app for Android, with an iOS version slated for 2026. The new app is packed with artificial intelligence features to help users manage their finances.

title: XRP stalls near $1.14 as breakout attempt struggles for volume
content: Buyers defended the session lows and pushed XRP back toward resistance, but muted overall volume left traders waiting for confirmation above $1.13-$1.14.
summary: XRP is struggling to break out above $1.13-$1.14 due to low trading volume. Buyers have defended the session lows and pushed the price back up, but confirmation is needed. The current price is near $1.14.

title: Cantor says Strategy's recovery hinges on restoring STRC to par
content: Bringing Strategy's preferred shares back to $100 is key to restarting Strategy's capital engine, the bank said, with repeated management actions expected to support both preferred and common shareholders.
summary: Cantor bank states that restoring Strategy's preferred shares to $100 is crucial for the company's recovery, which will support both preferred and common shareholders. This move is expected to restart Strategy's capital engine. The bank anticipates repeated management actions to achieve this goal.

title: A device that revives eyeballs from dead donors could make eye transplants possible
content: It’s not easy to transplant a whole human eye. The surgery is difficult. And the eyes themselves start to degenerate as soon as they’ve left the body. When surgeons attempted it a few years ago, the newly transplanted eye wasn’t able to see. But researchers believe they might have a solution: a device that maintains…
summary: Researchers have developed a device that can keep eyeballs from deceased donors alive, preventing degeneration that typically occurs after removal. This breakthrough could enable successful whole-eye transplants, a procedure that has previously failed due to the eye's rapid loss of function post-mortem.

I’m supporting it anyway. —Jessica Hamzelou As the parent of two little girls, I often think about how their childhood is different from…
summary: The Download newsletter edition covers a range of technology topics, including skepticism about the UK's generational tobacco ban and mentions a new story by author Elizabeth Bear.

title: The UK’s generational tobacco ban might not work. I’m supporting it anyway.
content: As the parent of two little girls, I often think about how their childhood is different from mine. The seven-year-old is learning about AI at school. The five-year-old is given internet-based homework every week. And they are both absolutely repulsed by the idea of smoking. That was not the prevailing sentiment when I was young.…
summary: The author, a parent of two young girls, reflects on how their generation is exposed to AI education and digital homework, while being strongly opposed to smoking. Despite doubts about its effectiveness, the author supports the UK's generational tobacco ban.

summary: Major brokerages initiated coverage on SpaceX with mostly buy ratings following the company's $75 billion June IPO. Analysts are optimistic, seeing more upside potential for the aerospace firm.

title: Why worms (and microbes) are catching on as a manure pollution solution
content: Anthony Agueda, a third-generation California dairy farmer, pulls a rake through a bed of dark, wet wood chips on his family’s land in Hickman, a tiny town in the state’s agricultural heartland. He reaches down with both hands and pulls up a clump of muck, turning it over to reveal a half-dozen squirming red earthworms.…
summary: A California dairy farmer is using worms and microbes to address manure pollution on his land, a potential solution for the agricultural industry. The method involves using earthworms to break down manure in wood chips. This approach could help reduce pollution and create a more sustainable farming practice.
